overview.about: Founded in 1753, the British Museum in London holds over 8 million objects spanning two million years of human history — the largest and most comprehensive collection of human culture on Earth. Its iconic Great Court, designed by Norman Foster, is the largest covered public square in Europe.

highlights.label: 8 Million Artefacts, 2M Years of History
highlights.desc: The largest collection of human culture on Earth — from Egyptian mummies to the Rosetta Stone in a single building

highlights.label: The Rosetta Stone
highlights.desc: The key to deciphering ancient Egyptian hieroglyphics — arguably the single most important artefact in recorded history

highlights.label: Norman Foster's Great Court
highlights.desc: The largest covered public square in Europe — 3,312 unique glass panes create a breathtaking geometric canopy

highlights.label: Free Entry Always
highlights.desc: Admission is permanently free for all visitors — one of the greatest cultural gifts any institution offers the world

overview.about: The Tower of London, founded in 1066 by William the Conqueror, is a UNESCO World Heritage castle complex on the north bank of the Thames. It served as a royal palace, prison, treasury and armoury over nine centuries. It holds the Crown Jewels — including the Imperial State Crown set with 2,868 diamonds.

highlights.label: The Crown Jewels
highlights.desc: 2,868 diamonds set into the Imperial State Crown — the most valuable and heavily guarded artefacts in Britain

highlights.label: 960 Years of Royal History
highlights.desc: Built by William the Conqueror in 1066 — served as palace, prison and mint across nine centuries of English monarchy

highlights.label: Beefeater Guided Tours
highlights.desc: Yeoman Warders (Beefeaters) lead daily tours packed with tales of execution, intrigue and royal drama

highlights.label: UNESCO World Heritage Site
highlights.desc: One of Britain's most iconic medieval fortress complexes — the White Tower alone is 900 years old

overview.about: Stonehenge, erected between 3000–1500 BC on Salisbury Plain, is the world's most famous prehistoric monument. Its massive sarsen stones, weighing up to 25 tonnes, were transported from 25 miles away using only Neolithic tools. Its precise astronomical alignment with solstice sunrises continues to mystify researchers.

highlights.label: 5,000-Year-Old Neolithic Wonder
highlights.desc: Constructed over 1,500 years between 3000–1500 BC — predating the Egyptian pyramids by 500 years

highlights.label: Summer Solstice Alignment
highlights.desc: The stones align precisely with the midsummer sunrise — proof of advanced astronomical knowledge in Neolithic Britons

highlights.label: UNESCO World Heritage
highlights.desc: Part of a wider prehistoric landscape including Avebury and 350+ burial mounds — the UK's most iconic heritage site

highlights.label: Ethereal at Dawn & Dusk
highlights.desc: Morning mist across Salisbury Plain at sunrise or sunset gives Stonehenge its most haunting and unforgettable atmosphere

overview.about: Edinburgh Castle, located in Edinburgh, GB, embodies Britain's profound layered history. From Roman foundations through medieval grandeur to Georgian elegance, this celebrated heritage landmark site tells the story of a small island that shaped global culture, law, science and language for centuries.

highlights.label: Centuries of Living History
highlights.desc: Structures standing for hundreds to thousands of years — each stone a silent chronicle of empires and civilisations

highlights.label: UNESCO World Heritage Site
highlights.desc: Recognised globally for outstanding universal value and protected as one of Earth's most significant cultural landmarks

highlights.label: Iconic Photography Spot
highlights.desc: Panoramic vistas, intricate carvings and sunrise silhouettes that appear on bucket lists worldwide — every angle tells a story

highlights.label: Architectural Masterpiece
highlights.desc: Precision engineering predating modern tools — symmetry, scale and craftsmanship that continues to baffle experts today

highlights.desc: World-class museums, galleries, theatres and street art that shape global trends in fashion, film and music

highlights.label: Legendary Cuisine & Markets
highlights.desc: A melting pot of culinary traditions — street food, fine dining and bustling markets all within easy reach

highlights.label: Vibrant Nightlife & Events
highlights.desc: Rooftop bars, jazz clubs, night markets and international festivals keep the city electric long after dark

highlights.label: Vibrant Nightlife & Events
highlights.desc: Rooftop bars, jazz clubs, night markets and international festivals keep the city electric long after dark

overview.about: Southern Edinburgh Beach, located in Edinburgh, GB, embodies Britain's profound layered history. From Roman foundations through medieval grandeur to Georgian elegance, this celebrated beach paradise site tells the story of a small island that shaped global culture, law, science and language for centuries.

highlights.label: Pristine Award-Winning Shores
highlights.desc: Crystal-clear azure waters fringing powder-white sands — consistently ranked among the world's most stunning coastlines

highlights.label: Premier Water Sports Hub
highlights.desc: Snorkelling, scuba diving, surfing and sailing in ideal conditions — equipment and instructors available year-round

highlights.label: Exceptional Sunset Views
highlights.desc: Catch the sky blaze in vivid gold and pink as the sun sets over the horizon — cited as a life-changing moment

highlights.label: Protected Marine Ecosystem
highlights.desc: Vibrant coral reefs, sea turtles and tropical fish — responsible tourism preserves these habitats for generations

overview.about: Northern Bushmills Summit, located in Bushmills, GB, embodies Britain's profound layered history. From Roman foundations through medieval grandeur to Georgian elegance, this celebrated mountain destination site tells the story of a small island that shaped global culture, law, science and language for centuries.

highlights.label: Awe-Inspiring Peaks
highlights.desc: Stand beneath peaks that pierce the clouds — some of the highest, most dramatic mountain ranges on the planet

highlights.label: World-Class Trekking Routes
highlights.desc: Legendary trails traversing alpine meadows, glacial passes and ancient shepherd paths used for centuries

highlights.label: Snow Adventures
highlights.desc: Skiing, snowboarding and ice-trekking on slopes that remain covered well into spring and summer months

highlights.label: Sunrise Panoramas
highlights.desc: Golden hour from high-altitude viewpoints paints surrounding valleys in colours no photograph fully captures

overview.about: Northern Amesbury Fortress, located in Amesbury, GB, embodies Britain's profound layered history. From Roman foundations through medieval grandeur to Georgian elegance, this celebrated heritage landmark site tells the story of a small island that shaped global culture, law, science and language for centuries.

highlights.label: Centuries of Living History
highlights.desc: Structures standing for hundreds to thousands of years — each stone a silent chronicle of empires and civilisations

highlights.label: UNESCO World Heritage Site
highlights.desc: Recognised globally for outstanding universal value and protected as one of Earth's most significant cultural landmarks

highlights.label: Iconic Photography Spot
highlights.desc: Panoramic vistas, intricate carvings and sunrise silhouettes that appear on bucket lists worldwide — every angle tells a story

highlights.label: Architectural Masterpiece
highlights.desc: Precision engineering predating modern tools — symmetry, scale and craftsmanship that continues to baffle experts today
